user_query:
I want to double-check a short Facebook post I wrote for our family before I share it, so I don’t accidentally spread anything untrue. Please use only the local reference files I saved under input/references to fact-check the draft at input/messages/facebook_post_draft.txt and then help me tidy the message for a warm, concise tone.

Requirements:
1) Inspect the input/references directory (do not hardcode filenames). Load every .json and .csv file you find there. In those files, each reference entry includes match_keywords. Treat a reference as matching a sentence in my draft if all of its match_keywords (case-insensitive) appear somewhere in that sentence.
2) Produce output/fact_check_report.csv with these columns for each sentence in the draft that asserts a factual claim: claim_sentence, matched_reference_ids (pipe-separated), consolidated_verdict (supported|contradicted|mixed|not found), sources (semicolon-separated source_title values), evidence_quotes (semicolon-separated quotes). If multiple matched references agree, use that verdict; if they disagree, set consolidated_verdict to mixed; if no references match a sentence, set not found.
3) Create output/facebook_post_annotated.md by copying the original text and appending to each factual sentence a bracketed tag like [CHECKED: SUPPORTED], [CHECKED: CONTRADICTED], [CHECKED: MIXED], or [CHECKED: NOT FOUND], based on your report. Leave greetings or non-factual chatter untagged.
4) Create output/post_rewrite.txt as a friendly, clear rewrite under 600 characters that keeps any supported fact(s), removes or gently corrects unsupported ones, and maintains a warm family tone. Keep the original files under input/ unchanged.

Notes:
- Please treat only sentences that make factual claims as claims (e.g., the lines about a free photo session, the Grandparents Day date, and lemon water curing colds). Questions or greetings need not be checked.
- Do not use the internet; rely solely on the references under input/references.
- All outputs must be written to the output/ directory listed above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
